title: cochlearia arctica subsp. oblongifolia
intro: Cochlearia arctica subsp. oblongifolia, also known as Arctic Cress or Sea Rocket, is a perennial plant species native to the Arctic regions, characterized by its bright green leaves and small white flowers. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects, due to the presence of compounds such as isothiocyanates and glucosinolates. The health benefits of Cochlearia arctica subsp. oblongifolia include its ability to support respiratory health, reduce inflammation, and boost the immune system, making it a popular choice in traditional herbal medicine. In terms of herbal preparations, the plant can be consumed as a tea, tincture, or infusions, often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
